headline: Fed News and Big Tech Earnings Collide This Week. The Mag 7 Matter Most.
summary: Wall Street is getting ready for one crazy Wednesday: A Federal Reserve meeting, a Senate committee vote on a new Fed chairman, and earnings reports from four Mag Seven companies after the closing bell.  The Cboe Volatility Index, also known as Wall Street’s fear gauge, has tumbled from above 30 in late March to a more reasonable level of 18.  Adding to the stakes: Never before have powerhouses  Alphabet Microsoft Amazon  and  Meta Platforms  all posted earnings on the same day.

Adding to the stakes: Never before have powerhouses  Alphabet Microsoft Amazon  and  Meta Platforms  all posted earnings on the same day.

headline: Big Tech investors to gauge payoff as AI spending set to hit $600 billion
summary: Big Tech has spent hundreds of billions of dollars over three years to power the artificial intelligence boom.  Quarterly results from Alphabet, Microsoft, ‌Meta and Amazon - all due on Wednesday - will gauge if the sky-high spending on AI has driven enough growth in cloud ‌computing and advertising to justify the cost.  Amazon and Instagram-parent Meta have announced job cuts affecting thousands of workers, while Microsoft has come up with its first employee buyout program in more than five decades.

Amazon and Instagram-parent Meta have announced job cuts affecting thousands of workers, while Microsoft has come up with its first employee buyout program in more than five decades.

headline: Bitcoin, XRP Prices Fall. What’s Holding Cryptos Back.
summary: Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies were falling early Tuesday as the stock market’s tech-driven rally looked set to hit pause.  Bitcoin  was trading at $76,637 early in the day, down 1.3% over the past 24 hours, according to CoinDesk data.  Nasdaq futures  slipped 0.4% ahead of the open after the index, and the S&P 500 eked out record closing highs.

content.title: Bitcoin, XRP Prices Fall. What’s Holding Cryptos Back.
content.summary: Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies were falling early Tuesday as the stock market’s tech-driven rally looked set to hit pause.  Bitcoin  was trading at $76,637 early in the day, down 1.3% over the past 24 hours, according to CoinDesk data.  Nasdaq futures  slipped 0.4% ahead of the open after the index, and the S&P 500 eked out record closing highs.

headline: UPS predicts return to growth after scaling down Amazon partnership: CFO explains
summary: While international shipper United Parcel Service (UPS) topped first quarter earnings and revenue estimates, its domestic package margins in the US may have fallen just shy. UPS stock is falling on Tuesday as the company affirms its full-year guidance. UPS CFO Brian Dykes sits down with Julie Hyman to  describe the freight operator's transformation as it draws down the size of its partnership with Amazon (AMZN). That includes the consolidation of logistics centers.

content.title: UPS predicts return to growth after scaling down Amazon partnership: CFO explains
content.description:
While international shipper United Parcel Service (UPS) topped first quarter earnings and revenue estimates, its domestic package margins in the US may have fallen just shy. UPS stock is falling on Tuesday as the company affirms its full-year guidance.

UPS CFO Brian Dykes sits down with Julie Hyman to  describe the freight operator's transformation as it draws down the size of its partnership with Amazon (AMZN). That includes the consolidation of logistics centers.
content.summary: While international shipper United Parcel Service (UPS) topped first quarter earnings and revenue estimates, its domestic package margins in the US may have fallen just shy. UPS stock is falling on Tuesday as the company affirms its full-year guidance. UPS CFO Brian Dykes sits down with Julie Hyman to  describe the freight operator's transformation as it draws down the size of its partnership with Amazon (AMZN). That includes the consolidation of logistics centers.

headline: Oracle Stock Dives on OpenAI Shock. CoreWeave, SoftBank Hit as Fears Rise.
summary: Oracle  stock was falling Tuesday as its relationship with ChatGPT-developer OpenAI is under scrutiny again—and taking down much of the AI trade with it.  Its shares have fallen 15% in 2026 over concerns about its capital expenditures as it builds out artificial-intelligence data centers.  The trigger for Tuesday’s slide is a report in the The Wall Street Journal that OpenAI has recently missed its own targets for new users and revenue, leading to concerns among executives about whether it will be able to fulfill its spending commitments.

content.title: Oracle Stock Dives on OpenAI Shock. CoreWeave, SoftBank Hit as Fears Rise.
content.summary: Oracle  stock was falling Tuesday as its relationship with ChatGPT-developer OpenAI is under scrutiny again—and taking down much of the AI trade with it.  Its shares have fallen 15% in 2026 over concerns about its capital expenditures as it builds out artificial-intelligence data centers.  The trigger for Tuesday’s slide is a report in the The Wall Street Journal that OpenAI has recently missed its own targets for new users and revenue, leading to concerns among executives about whether it will be able to fulfill its spending commitments.

headline: A Midweek Macro-Storm of Warsh, Fed, and Big Tech Could Create a Volatility Vortex
summary: Wall Street is getting ready for one crazy Wednesday: A Federal Reserve meeting, a Senate committee vote on a new Fed chairman, and earnings reports from four Mag Seven companies after the closing bell.  The Cboe Volatility Index, also known as Wall Street’s fear gauge, has tumbled from above 30 in late March to a more reasonable level of 18.  Adding to the stakes: Never before have powerhouses  Alphabet Microsoft Amazon  and  Meta Platforms  all posted earnings on the same day.

headline: EU rules reining in Big Tech will now target cloud services and AI, regulators say
summary: The European Union plans to turn the focus of its landmark rules curbing the power of Big Tech to cloud and artificial ‌intelligence services, aiming to promote fairer competition after seeing positive results in other digital areas, ‌EU regulators said.  The EU Commission's Digital Markets Act currently targets Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Booking.com, ByteDance, Meta Platforms and Microsoft, imposing obligations ​on so-called "gatekeepers" to prevent them from abusing their market dominance.  The Commission said the DMA, which became applicable in May 2023, has improved conditions for businesses and users.

content.title: EU rules reining in Big Tech will now target cloud services and AI, regulators say
content.summary: The European Union plans to turn the focus of its landmark rules curbing the power of Big Tech to cloud and artificial ‌intelligence services, aiming to promote fairer competition after seeing positive results in other digital areas, ‌EU regulators said.  The EU Commission's Digital Markets Act currently targets Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Booking.com, ByteDance, Meta Platforms and Microsoft, imposing obligations ​on so-called "gatekeepers" to prevent them from abusing their market dominance.  The Commission said the DMA, which became applicable in May 2023, has improved conditions for businesses and users.

headline: Oracle Stock Is Paying the Price for OpenAI’s Failures
summary: Oracle  stock was falling Tuesday as its relationship with ChatGPT-developer OpenAI is under scrutiny again—and taking down much of the AI trade with it even though the tech start-up has pushed back.  Its shares have fallen 15% in 2026 over concerns about its capital expenditures as it builds out artificial-intelligence data centers.  The trigger for Tuesday’s slide is a report in the The Wall Street Journal that OpenAI has recently missed its own targets for new users and revenue, leading to concerns among executives about whether it will be able to fulfill its spending commitments.

content.title: Oracle Stock Is Paying the Price for OpenAI’s Failures
content.summary: Oracle  stock was falling Tuesday as its relationship with ChatGPT-developer OpenAI is under scrutiny again—and taking down much of the AI trade with it even though the tech start-up has pushed back.  Its shares have fallen 15% in 2026 over concerns about its capital expenditures as it builds out artificial-intelligence data centers.  The trigger for Tuesday’s slide is a report in the The Wall Street Journal that OpenAI has recently missed its own targets for new users and revenue, leading to concerns among executives about whether it will be able to fulfill its spending commitments.

headline: Amazon touts a 'major expansion' with OpenAI as Microsoft ties loosen
summary: Amazon announced what it called a “major expansion” of its partnership with ChatGPT maker OpenAI on Tuesday, a day after the artificial intelligence company said it was loosening its ties to longtime backer Microsoft.  OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said the collaboration with Amazon's cloud computing division, Amazon Web Services, would involve co-developing a new platform for AI agents that can do computer-based work on people's behalf.  Altman spoke via prerecorded video message to an Amazon event in San Francisco at the same time as he was appearing in federal court across San Francisco Bay in Oakland for a civil trial brought by rival OpenAI co-founder Elon Musk.

content.title: Amazon touts a 'major expansion' with OpenAI as Microsoft ties loosen
content.summary: Amazon announced what it called a “major expansion” of its partnership with ChatGPT maker OpenAI on Tuesday, a day after the artificial intelligence company said it was loosening its ties to longtime backer Microsoft.  OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said the collaboration with Amazon's cloud computing division, Amazon Web Services, would involve co-developing a new platform for AI agents that can do computer-based work on people's behalf.  Altman spoke via prerecorded video message to an Amazon event in San Francisco at the same time as he was appearing in federal court across San Francisco Bay in Oakland for a civil trial brought by rival OpenAI co-founder Elon Musk.

thumbnail.caption: Matt Garman, CEO of AWS, left, speaks next to Denise Dresser, chief revenue officer of OpenAI, at a What's Next with AWS event Tuesday, April 28, 2026, in San Francisco. (AP Photo/Jeff Chiu)
